1.The SIC (Standard Industrial Classification) code is a 5-digit code that identifies what a business does. For example SIC code 01120 is the code for the growing of rice. The current SIC codes were issued in 2007 and replaced the older SIC 2003 codes. HSBC introduced SIC 2007 codes from 01/01/2011 in order to comply with Bank of England reporting requirements. 

2.The archive.Customeryymm table will hold the SIC code held on Browser Main Menu at the time the data was cut from SAMS
column3: Industry code

column2:
1.The table should be used as a lookup to obtain full text descriptions of the industry classification of each SIC code stored in the SIC field of Archive Customer Table

2.Can be used to support analysis that groups data at an industry level.

3.The FACTORABLE_STATUS field can be used to identify customers in an industry with a propensity for invoice finance.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 4. industry_desc also known as SECTOR
column3: Industry, Industry description

column2:
A form of credit risk rating for portfolio managed customers. Generally a high score equals low risk and a low score equals high risk, with the following exceptions:
Behavioural Score sourced from SAMS

1 = Customer for >1 month but no score calculated yet
4 = Debit Dormant
5 = Complex Customers
30 = Categorised Lending
column3: Behaviour score, Behave score of the customer, customer rating

column2:
Field identifying the point at which the starting date at which the record is live within the archive.

The record relates to the first day of the month for the archive refresh. E.g. If the archive for 1409 data is refreshed in October. When the table is updated, the value would be given as 01SEP2014
column3: IMIS Product Start Date, IMIS Product  Open Date

column2:
Field identifying the date at which the record stopped being live within the archive.

The record relates to the first day of the month following the  records closure E.g. If the archive for 1409 data is refreshed in October, and this record closes. When the table is updated, the value would be given as 01SEP2014
column3: IMIS Product End Date, IMIS Product  Closure Date
